official_high3.description: High-school year 3 official exam baseline with transparent vocabulary and clear logic.

official_high3_hard.description: High-school year 3 hard official mode: similar length, higher abstraction and clue distance.

indepth_high3.description: High-school year 3 in-depth mode: compressed but more abstract, with stronger distractor traps.

middle3_official.description: Middle-school official family, also used as the default school-style family for middle1 and middle2.

middle3_bridge.description: Bridge family toward high1 with 0.85~0.95 length and slightly higher inference load.

middle1.description: Short, direct, concrete, with the key clue inside one sentence.

middle2.description: Slightly longer with basic inference beginning to appear.

middle3_official.description: Official middle3 style close to entry-level high1, but with direct connectors preserved.

middle3_bridge.description: Bridge style toward high1 at 0.85~0.95 length with compressed 31/36/38 variants allowed.

H1.description: Standard-centered with explicit connectors and partial restatement distractors.

H2.description: Hard-centered with referent tracking and stronger penalties for direct clues.

H3.description: Hard to killer with more abstraction, paraphrase distance, and compressed logic.

practical_notice.description: Instructional or notice-like language with short surface cues.

email_letter.description: Personal address, sender-receiver framing, explicit discourse connectors.

biography.description: Event progression with chronology and reference tracking.

expository.description: Informational explanation with explicit logic markers.

narrative.description: Story-like progression with reference chaining and temporal cues.

dialogue.description: Turn-based exchange with shorter utterances and local context setup.

academic_argument.description: Claim-evidence-rebuttal structure with dense transitions and abstract nouns.

mixed_expository_narrative.description: Hybrid passage blending exposition and episode-based support.
